from pyspark.sql import functions as F, types as T
import uuid

# Horizon extract (has coEmailAddress)
HZN_USERS_PATH = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-EXTRACT/Users.csv"

# Entra folder (your new folder)
ENTRA_FOLDER_PATH = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-TRANSFORM/Entra/"

# Outputs
OUT_MATCHED_DEBUG = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-TRANSFORM/UserEntraMatchDebug/"
OUT_USERS_FOR_LOAD = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-TRANSFORM/UserWithEntraId/"

hzn = (spark.read
    .option("header", "true")
    .csv(HZN_USERS_PATH)
)

hzn = (hzn
    .withColumn("coEmailAddress", F.trim(F.col("coEmailAddress")))
    .withColumn("hzn_email_raw", F.lower(F.col("coEmailAddress")))
    .withColumn("hzn_email_local", F.split(F.col("hzn_email_raw"), "@").getItem(0))
)

print("Horizon rows:", hzn.count())
display(hzn.limit(20))

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

ENTRA_FOLDER_PATH = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-TRANSFORM/Entra/"

# 1) Try multiline JSON first (many Graph dumps are single JSON documents per file)
entra_raw = (spark.read
    .option("multiline", "true")
    .json(ENTRA_FOLDER_PATH)
)

# If Spark still can't parse, it will have only _corrupt_record
only_corrupt = (entra_raw.columns == ["_corrupt_record"]) or (set(entra_raw.columns) == {"_corrupt_record"})

if not only_corrupt:
    print("✅ Parsed as JSON. Columns:", entra_raw.columns)
else:
    print("⚠️ JSON parse failed; falling back to TEXT parsing...")

    # 2) Fallback: read as text, pull out every "value": [...] block, parse those as JSON arrays
    txt = spark.read.text(ENTRA_FOLDER_PATH)

    # Concatenate all file text into one string
    blob = txt.agg(F.concat_ws("\n", F.collect_list("value")).alias("blob")).collect()[0]["blob"]

    # Extract each value-array inside pages: "value":[ ... ]
    # This regex pulls the inside of the value array (non-greedy)
    import re
    chunks = re.findall(r'"value"\s*:\s*\[(.*?)\]\s*(?:,|\})', blob, flags=re.DOTALL)

    if len(chunks) == 0:
        raise Exception("Could not find any \"value\": [...] arrays in Entra export text.")

    # Turn each chunk into a valid JSON array string
    arrays = ["[" + c + "]" for c in chunks]

    # Parse arrays into rows (each row is one user object)
    rdd = spark.sparkContext.parallelize(arrays)
    pages = spark.read.json(rdd)   # rows are user objects now
    entra_raw = pages

    print(f"✅ Fallback parsed users. Rows: {entra_raw.count()}")
    print("Columns:", entra_raw.columns)

# At this point entra_raw should be either:
# - page objects with a `value` array, OR
# - already user objects

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

entra = entra_raw.select(F.explode("value").alias("u")).select("u.*")

print("Entra user columns:", entra.columns)
print("Entra user rows:", entra.count())

# ensure optional columns exist
if "mail" not in entra.columns:
    entra = entra.withColumn("mail", F.lit(None).cast("string"))
if "userPrincipalName" not in entra.columns:
    entra = entra.withColumn("userPrincipalName", F.lit(None).cast("string"))
if "otherMails" not in entra.columns:
    entra = entra.withColumn("otherMails", F.array().cast("array<string>"))

entra_keyed = (entra
    .withColumn(
        "entra_email_raw",
        F.lower(F.trim(F.coalesce(
            F.col("mail"),
            F.col("userPrincipalName"),
            F.element_at(F.col("otherMails"), 1)  # first element (1-indexed)
        )))
    )
    .withColumn("entra_email_local", F.split(F.col("entra_email_raw"), "@").getItem(0))
    .select(
        F.col("id").alias("idpUserId"),
        "entra_email_raw",
        "entra_email_local",
        "mail",
        "userPrincipalName",
        "displayName"
    )
)

entra_dedup = entra_keyed.dropDuplicates(["entra_email_local"])

print("Entra keyed rows:", entra_keyed.count())
print("Entra dedup rows:", entra_dedup.count())
display(entra_dedup.limit(20))

hzn_users_path = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net/MPESC-EXTRACT/Users.csv"

hzn = (spark.read.option("header","true").csv(hzn_users_path)
    .withColumn("coEmailAddress", F.trim(F.col("coEmailAddress")))
    .withColumn("hzn_email_raw", F.lower(F.col("coEmailAddress")))
    .withColumn("hzn_email_local", F.split(F.col("hzn_email_raw"), "@").getItem(0))
)

print("Horizon users rows:", hzn.count())
display(hzn.limit(20))

matched = (hzn.join(
        entra_dedup,
        hzn["hzn_email_local"] == entra_dedup["entra_email_local"],
        how="left"
    )
    .select(
        "caseOfficerId",
        "caseOfficerLogin",
        "caseOfficerName",
        "coEmailAddress",
        "idpUserId",
        "userPrincipalName",
        "mail",
        "displayName"
    )
)

display(matched.orderBy("caseOfficerName").limit(200))
print("Matched:", matched.filter(F.col("idpUserId").isNotNull()).count())
print("Unmatched:", matched.filter(F.col("idpUserId").isNull()).count())

import uuid
from pyspark.sql import types as T
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

NAMESPACE_UUID = uuid.UUID("6ba7b810-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")

@F.udf(returnType=T.StringType())
def uuid5_from_legacy(legacy_id: str):
    if legacy_id is None:
        return None
    return str(uuid.uuid5(NAMESPACE_UUID, legacy_id))

users_for_load = (matched
    .withColumn("legacyId", F.col("caseOfficerId").cast("string"))
    .withColumn("id", uuid5_from_legacy(F.col("legacyId")))
    .select(
        "id",
        "idpUserId",
        "legacyId",
        "caseOfficerLogin",
        "coEmailAddress",
        "caseOfficerName"
    )
    .dropDuplicates(["id"])
)

display(users_for_load.orderBy("caseOfficerName").limit(200))

print("Rows ready:", users_for_load.count())
print("Missing idpUserId:", users_for_load.filter(F.col("idpUserId").isNull()).count())

# Remove users with NULL idpUserId
users_for_load = users_for_load.filter(F.col("idpUserId").isNotNull())

print("After removing NULL idpUserId:")
print("Rows ready:", users_for_load.count())

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# Add flag column
users_all = (
    users_for_load
    .withColumn(
        "hasEntraId",
        F.when(F.col("idpUserId").isNotNull(), F.lit(True)).otherwise(F.lit(False))
    )
)

# Output path
BASE_PATH = "abfss://horizon-migration-poc@pinsstodwdevuks9h80mb.dfs.core.windows.net"
OUT_USERS_CSV = f"{BASE_PATH}/MPESC-TRANSFORM/UserAll_WithEntraFlag/"

# Save as single CSV
(users_all
    .coalesce(1)
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .option("header", "true")
    .csv(OUT_USERS_CSV)
)

print("Saved combined CSV to:", OUT_USERS_CSV)
print("Total users:", users_all.count())
print("With Entra:", users_all.filter(F.col("hasEntraId")).count())
print("Without Entra:", users_all.filter(~F.col("hasEntraId")).count())
